name: Chhattisgarh
avoid: Remote interior forest areas of southern Bastar, especially outside marked tourism zones, should be planned only after checking current local advisories; Jagdalpur, Chitrakote, Kanger Valley and Dantewada remain standard, well-visited tourism circuits.
stopoverNote: Raipur is the practical air gateway. The Raipur→Jagdalpur/Bastar road leg is a long 295–300 km, 6–7 hr drive via Kanker/Kondagaon; night buses save time, but self-drives after dark on forest roads are not recommended.

diet.vegetarian: Chhattisgarh is easy for vegetarians: rice-based chila, faraa/phara, bafauri, muthiya, dubki kadhi, angakar roti and seasonal saag are common, with veg thalis widely available in Raipur and Jagdalpur. Dairy and ghee are common, so ask before ordering.
diet.vegan: Many local staples can be vegan if cooked in oil: chila, faraa, bafauri, muthiya, angakar roti and vegetable bhaji. Dedicated vegan restaurants are rare outside Raipur, so request 'bina ghee, doodh, dahi' and carry snacks on Bastar road days.
phrases: Bina doodh/dahi = without milk or curd
Sirf tel mein banao = cook only in oil

links.label: Kanger Valley National Park Official Tariff

cities.name: Raipur
cities.notes: Chhattisgarh's capital and sole major air gateway — a practical first-day base for tribal heritage, museums, zoo-safari and local food before the Bastar road leg.

cities.name: Sirpur
cities.notes: A quiet 5th–8th century archaeological complex by the Mahanadi with Buddhist monasteries, Hindu temples and Jain remains, usually done as a Raipur day trip.

cities.name: Jagdalpur / Bastar
cities.notes: The offbeat heart of the trip — Chitrakote Falls, Kanger Valley caves, Tirathgarh Falls, Danteshwari Temple, tribal haats and Bastar Dussehra culture.

itinerary.sim: Any Indian prepaid SIM works; Jio and Airtel are strongest around Raipur, Sirpur and Jagdalpur. Download offline maps before Bastar forest drives because coverage can turn patchy inside Kanger Valley and remote village roads.

Raipur.name: Raipur
Raipur.note: Use Raipur as the arrival base and cultural orientation stop before heading east to Sirpur and south to Bastar.

days.theme: Capital orientation — culture, safari and local food
days.note: All stops are within a short city drive; avoid the outdoor sites at midday in Apr–Jun and note that the zoo/safari and Purkhouti Muktangan close on Mondays.

activities.name: Purkhouti Muktangan open-air tribal heritage museum
activities.tip: Best first stop for understanding Chhattisgarh's folk, tribal and craft context before Bastar. Open roughly 8am–6pm; closed Monday.

activities.name: Mahant Ghasidas Memorial Museum
activities.tip: One of the state's oldest museums, with tribal artefacts, natural history and prehistoric finds; verify current government hours.

activities.name: Nandan Van Zoo & Safari
activities.tip: Good family stop for white tiger and Asiatic lion exhibits. Arrive by 3pm to allow safari time; closed Monday.

activities.name: Telibandha Talab / Marine Drive evening walk
activities.tip: Pleasant lake promenade for sunset and street snacks; try chila or local samosas nearby.

meals: Chila and faraa at local breakfast stalls

Sirpur.name: Sirpur
Sirpur.note: A full day is enough for the archaeological zone; most travelers day-trip from Raipur or stay near Mahasamund for early light.

days.theme: Ancient Sirpur — Buddhist monasteries, temples and the Mahanadi
days.note: The site is compact and walkable but exposed; leave Raipur around 7:30am, carry water/snacks and aim for morning light before the fields heat up.

activities.name: Lakshmana Temple and Surang Tila archaeological complex
activities.tip: The 7th-century brick Lakshmana Temple and tiered Surang Tila are the headline monuments; start before 10am for cooler weather.

activities.name: Sirpur Buddhist monasteries — Anand Prabha Vihara and Swastika Vihara
activities.tip: Look for ASI labels, monastery layouts and inscriptions from the 5th–8th century excavations.

activities.name: Sirpur Museum / site sculpture collection
activities.cost: Free or nominal — verify at gate
activities.tip: Small but useful context for sculptures, coins and Buddhist finds excavated from the complex.

activities.name: Mahanadi River ghat walk
activities.tip: A peaceful riverside finish; occasional boatmen may offer short local rides.

meals: Carry snacks and water from Raipur
Simple veg thali on the Raipur return

Jagdalpur / Bastar.name: Jagdalpur / Bastar
Jagdalpur / Bastar.note: Three nights is the sweet spot for Chitrakote, Kanger Valley and a Dantewada/tribal-culture day. Waterfalls peak Jul–Oct, but Oct–Mar gives the best balance of flow, access and weather.

days.note: After the long Raipur/Sirpur transfer, keep the day focused on Jagdalpur arrival and the 38 km run to Chitrakote for afternoon light.

activities.name: Chitrakote Falls on the Indravati River
activities.tip: India's widest waterfall. Post-monsoon Sep–Oct has the biggest flow; Oct–Jan balances water volume and access. Carry a waterproof bag for the base boat ride.

activities.name: Jagdalpur local market and Bastar handicrafts browse
activities.tip: Look for dhokra bell-metal, wrought iron and terracotta; Kondagaon is the deeper craft hub if you have extra time.

activities.name: Evening at Dalpat Sagar / city food crawl
activities.tip: Keep dinner simple after the road leg; ask for chila, faraa or a local thali.

hotels.name: Bastar Jungle Resort / Bastar Greens Eco Stays
River Park Resort / Swastik Anandam near Chitrakote

days.theme: Kanger Valley — caves, forest and Tirathgarh Falls
days.note: Start early. Kutumsar/Kailash caves are seasonal and usually closed in monsoon; entry cutoffs are early, and Kutumsar has narrow, slippery sections unsuitable for claustrophobic travelers.

activities.name: Kutumsar Cave — stalactites and blind cave fish
activities.cost: ₹25 Indians / ₹150 foreigners; forest jeep ₹1,500 shared; guide ₹300 for 8 persons
activities.tip: Official Kanger Valley tariff applies. Closed shoes and a small torch help; guide is mandatory.

activities.name: Tirathgarh Falls inside Kanger Valley National Park
activities.tip: Multi-tier waterfall in lush forest, generally open 8am–6pm. Best Oct–Feb for flow and safe access.

activities.name: Kailash Cave and Kanger Dhara nature stop
activities.cost: Covered by park entry; jeep/guide charges may apply
activities.tip: Combine with Kutumsar if timings work; Kanger Dhara is a gentle stream/birding stop.

activities.name: Anthropological Museum, Jagdalpur
activities.tip: Evening context on Bastar communities, instruments, costumes and ritual objects.

hotels.name: Bastar Jungle Resort / Bastar Greens Eco Stays

meals: Simple dhaba lunch near park gates

days.theme: Dantewada, Bastar culture and tribal crafts
days.note: Dantewada is ~82 km from Jagdalpur and makes a long but rewarding half/full-day excursion. Confirm weekly haat locations locally because schedules rotate by village.

activities.name: Danteshwari Temple, Dantewada
activities.tip: A revered Shakti Peetha and the spiritual heart of Bastar Dussehra; morning aarti is atmospheric. Dress modestly.

activities.name: Bastar Palace and Dussehra chariot context
activities.tip: The 600-year Bastar Dussehra tradition centers on goddess Danteshwari rather than a Ravana effigy; book Jagdalpur stays 6–8 weeks ahead for the October climax.

activities.name: Weekly tribal haat or Kondagaon dhokra craft village
activities.tip: Ask your hotel/guide for the nearest market day. Buy bell-metal, wrought iron or terracotta directly from artisans where possible.

activities.name: Optional return stop at a local waterfall viewpoint
activities.tip: Use only marked tourism zones and daylight hours; do not detour into remote forest interiors without local confirmation.

connections.method: Road by private taxi/rented car via Abhanpur and Mahasamund
Long road transfer; recommended practical routing is Sirpur→Raipur overnight, then Raipur→Jagdalpur by bus/taxi
connections.cost: Raipur→Jagdalpur bus ₹515–₹900; sedan taxi ₹4,000–₹4,600 one way; SUV ₹6,300–₹9,000

connections.method: Return by NH30 bus/taxi or overnight AC sleeper bus

extras: Barnawapara Wildlife Sanctuary is a worthwhile optional Raipur-side add-on from November to June only; it closes July–October for monsoon. Budget roughly ₹55–₹100/person entry, ₹1,300–₹1,800 per gypsy safari and ₹200–₹250 compulsory guide.

Waterfalls are most dramatic July–October, especially Chitrakote, but first-time travelers should prefer September/October to March because caves, roads and forest access are more reliable outside peak monsoon.
Bastar Dussehra 2026 runs roughly mid-July to around October 20, with the October climax in Jagdalpur; accommodation can sell out weeks ahead.
The Raipur–Jagdalpur leg is the itinerary's main feasibility constraint: allow 7+ hours with breaks, download offline maps, and avoid unplanned post-dark forest-road driving.

Interior/deep forest zones in parts of southern Bastar such as Bijapur and Sukma are not standard tourism circuits; verify current conditions with local operators or official advisories before venturing beyond marked sites.
Most quoted entry fees are verified from 2025–2026 sources and can change at the gate; carry cash for small tickets, parking, guides and boats.
